topic: §6.14 pointer — c1 hole 2 verification ('geometry weight' → 'FSF' alignment with §4.2.5)
change_summary: c1 hole 2 (terminology drift) patched: changed 'geometry weight' to 'FSF' in §6.14 pointer to align with §4.2.5 destination term.
our_argument_summary: §6.14 pointer paragraph cross-references §4.2.5 for the magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination argument. Pre-patch text used the plain-English gloss 'geometry weight'; destination §4.2.5 uses the formally-named 'FSF' (Footprint-Scaling Factor). Patch substitutes FSF in the pointer for terminology consistency. C1 also had hole 1 (pass-band capitalization) which was independently self-applied — see queue 434/435.

c1_hole2_geometry_weight_to_FSF.current_text: The magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination argument — that (baseline × coverage × FSF) is shared arithmetic between the dome's W004 recalibration and any coverage-scaled Chapman-Sq-dynamo treatment — lives at §4.2.5
c1_hole2_geometry_weight_to_FSF.destination_alignment: data/sections.json:part4.html §4.2.5: 'The eclipse formula ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF'
c1_hole2_geometry_weight_to_FSF.check: Pointer parenthetical and destination formula now use the same term ('FSF'). Cross-reference now reads consistently.

c1_hole1_pass_band_lowercase_also_applie.note: Hole 1 (pass-band capitalization) was self-applied in parallel — pointer now reads 'magnitude-only pass-band non-discrimination' (lowercase). Already verified in queue 434/435 reviews; recorded here for completeness.

holes_found.description: Asymmetry note (overlap with part6.c1 hole #1 of the batched review for queue 435): the FSF terminology fix landed in part6.html §6.14 pointer (this queue item's verification scope) but the parallel arithmetic-decomposition prose in part8b.html §8b.3 still uses 'geometry weight' (2 instances at idx 7539 and 14569). Both part8b instances are in the exact (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) parenthetical pattern that §4.2.5 codifies as FSF. The §6.14 pointer's destination consistency is achieved; the broader cross-section consistency is not (part8b drifts). This is duplicated in queue 435's part6.c1 review for visibility; not blocking queue 437 closure.
holes_found.recommendation: See part6.c1 (queue 435 review) for the part8b follow-up recommendation. No new action needed here — handled by the parallel review's recommended follow-up issue.

holes_found.description: Tracking hygiene: ISS-2425 (the FSF terminology issue this queue item verifies) is still listed status='open' in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json despite the patch having landed in sections.json. Same finding flagged in part6.c1 (queue 435).
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should close ISS-2425 with a resolution field citing the patch commit. (Same recommendation as part6.c1 hole #2 — single follow-up closes both.)

kernel_of_truth.description: ISS-2425's diagnosis was precise: a cross-reference pointer should use the same vocabulary as its destination, especially when the destination explicitly names a term ('FSF' is defined as 'Footprint-Scaling Factor' in part4 FORM-V51-005). The patch does exactly that — replaces the plain-English gloss with the defined term (data/sections.json:part6.html §6.14 pointer; part4.html FORM-V51-005 'FSF Station Scaling').
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The patch is correct within its scope (part6 →§4.2.5 cross-reference). The motivation — consistency across cross-references to the same arithmetic decomposition — applies equally to part8b.html §8b.3 (idx 7539, 14569) where the same (baseline × coverage × geometry weight) parenthetical still appears. A defender comparing §8b.3 and §4.2.5 sees the drift ISS-2425 was created to fix; the §6.14 patch addresses only one of the two cross-section pointers (data/sections.json:part8b.html idx 7539 and 14569).

citations_verified: data/sections.json:part4.html §4.2.5 destination: 'The eclipse formula ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF'
data/sections.json:part4.html FORM-V51-005: 'FSF Station Scaling' (FSF is a defined term in the formula registry)

summary_for_decider: Queue 437 (SEC-part6-EXP462-P1, class=verification, c2): The FSF terminology patch landed cleanly in §6.14 pointer. Destination consistency with §4.2.5 achieved. Two minor holes (both duplicates of part6.c1 findings — single follow-up resolves all): (1) part8b.html §8b.3 still uses 'geometry weight' in the parallel parenthetical; (2) ISS-2425 still status='open' in open-issues.json. Recommended action: no_change for this queue item; pop queue 437 closed.
